maxContractTerm.value: 5 years maximum. Bus contracts between districts and private bus contractors may run for a period of more than one year but not exceeding five years (Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.108(1)). The implementing statute (MCA 20-10-124) does not itself set a numeric term limit; the 5-year cap is fixed by the Board of Public Education's administrative rule, promulgated under the authority of MCA 20-3-106 and 20-10-112 and implementing MCA 20-10-124 and 20-10-143. ARM 10.7.108(1) further requires that bus contracts be signed prior to the trustees' adoption of the budget, and MCA 20-10-124(2) requires that transportation contracts for the ensuing year be completed by the fourth Monday of June.
maxContractTerm.citation: Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.108(1); see also MCA 20-10-124(2)

rebidRequired.value: Conditional. Before awarding a school bus transportation contract with a private party, trustees must EITHER (a) secure competitive bids by publishing at least three calls for bids over a 21-day period in a newspaper of the county and let the contract to the lowest responsible bidder (trustees retain the right to reject any and all bids), OR (b) negotiate a new contract with the current school bus contractor, but only if the negotiated contract costs do not exceed by more than 12% per year the basic costs of the previous year's contract, and only at a public meeting of the trustees at which district patrons may appear and be heard (notice of the meeting must be published in a newspaper of wide circulation within the district at least 1 week prior). Bidding is therefore mandatory unless the statutory negotiation safe harbor in MCA 20-10-125(1)(b) is satisfied. The bid-letting process is also subject to the conflict-of-interest and contract-letting provisions of MCA 20-9-204.

renewalMechanics.value: A new contract with the current contractor may be negotiated (in lieu of a competitive rebid) only if the negotiated cost does not exceed by more than 12% per year the basic costs of the previous year's contract, only at a public trustee meeting where district patrons may appear and be heard, and only after at least 1 week's prior published notice of the meeting in a newspaper of wide circulation within the district (MCA 20-10-125(1)(b)). The underlying contract itself may have a term of more than one year up to a 5-year maximum (ARM 10.7.108(1)). All transportation contracts (new or renewed) must be signed prior to adoption of the trustees' budget and completed by the fourth Monday of June for the ensuing year (ARM 10.7.108(1); MCA 20-10-124(2)). No automatic/evergreen rollover, CPI/index escalator, fuel-adjustment clause, or state-agency renewal-approval mechanism is provided by statute or rule. The only statutorily authorized renewal-without-rebid mechanism is the 12%/year negotiated-renewal cap in MCA 20-10-125(1)(b).

statutoryCitation.value: Primary statutes: MCA 20-10-124 (Private party contract for transportation -- individual transportation contract) and MCA 20-10-125 (Bid letting for contract bus -- payments under transportation contract). Primary administrative rule: Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.108 (Bus Contracts, fixing the 5-year maximum term); see also Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.105 (Pupil Transportation Contracts). The Montana Procurement Act (Title 18, Chapter 4 MCA) does NOT apply to school districts of its own force -- MCA 18-4-124(1) provides that 'A political subdivision or school district may adopt any or all parts of this chapter and the accompanying rules,' making Title 18 Chapter 4 optional for districts. The binding authority for student-transportation contracting is therefore the school-specific Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1 MCA framework and ARM Chapter 10.7, Subchapter 1.

districtVariance.value: Unknown. No Montana attorney-general opinion, OPI bulletin, state-auditor finding, or MTSBA model-policy document was located that demonstrates district-by-district variance in the interpretation of MCA 20-10-124, 20-10-125, or ARM 10.7.108. OPI's Pupil Transportation page (opi.mt.gov/Leadership/Finance-Grants/School-Finance/Pupil-Transportation) directs districts uniformly to Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1 MCA and ARM Chapter 10.7, Subchapter 1, and OPI publishes uniform TR-1, TR-4, and TR-5 reporting forms and the OPI-compiled 'School Laws of Montana 2025'; this suggests centralized OPI administration but is not affirmative evidence of uniform statutory interpretation across districts. MTSBA model district policy language recites the 21-day three-calls-for-bids and 12%-negotiation framework, suggesting widespread uniform district adoption, but no document affirmatively certifies statewide uniformity. Negative search path: searched OPI 'School Finance Compass' bulletins, MTSBA policy services, dojmt.gov / Montana DOJ AG opinion database (courts.mt.gov/external/ag-opinions), and sfsd.mt.gov Local Government Services audit findings -- no district-variance evidence located.

specialEducation.value: No separate Montana statutory or administrative contracting framework for special-education transportation was located. MCA Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1 and ARM Chapter 10.7 apply uniformly to all district-contracted pupil transportation, including transportation furnished to students with disabilities. The IEP-related duty to provide transportation as a 'related service' is governed by federal IDEA and incorporated into Montana law through MCA Title 20, Chapter 7, Part 4 (Special Education for Exceptional Children), but those provisions create a duty to transport -- they do not modify the contract-term, bidding, or renewal rules in MCA 20-10-124, 20-10-125, or ARM 10.7.108. ARM Chapter 10.16 (Special Education) addresses identification, IEP processes, due-process hearings, tuition rates, and records; it does not create a separate procurement framework for district-contractor school-bus contracts. Medicaid-billed school-based specialized transportation under ARM 37.86.2234 (EPSDT) involves separate Medicaid provider-enrollment and billing requirements but does not create a different procurement framework for district-contractor school-bus contracts. ARM 10.61.207 addresses transportation specifically for residential students of the Montana School for the Deaf and Blind and likewise does not modify the general Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 / ARM Ch. 10.7 procurement rules. Therefore the 5-year max term (ARM 10.7.108), the competitive-bid-or-12%-negotiated-renewal rule (MCA 20-10-125), and the contract-completion mechanics (MCA 20-10-124) apply equally to special-education transportation contracts that a district lets with a private contractor.
specialEducation.citation: MCA 20-10-124; MCA 20-10-125; Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.108; cf. MCA Title 20, Chapter 7, Part 4 (duty-to-provide only); cf. ARM 37.86.2234 (Medicaid EPSDT, billing only)

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term = 5 years (ARM 10.7.108(1)); MCA 20-10-124 silent on term length.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A longer-term, multi-year, successive, extension, or 'notwithstanding' authority exists in MCA Title 20, Ch. 10 or related procurement law that overrides ARM 10.7.108's 5-year cap; or ARM 10.7.108 has been amended after Stage 1's source date to alter the term.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-fetched MCA 20-10-124 at mca.legmt.gov (no term language present). Re-fetched MCA 20-10-110 (deals with bus PURCHASE installment contracts, 3-year max, NOT services). Read MCA Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 sections_index to confirm no other section sets a service-contract term cap. Confirmed ARM 10.7.108 verbatim text via Cornell LII pathfinder ('not exceeding five years'); history line shows last amendment 6/26/1998 (no post-1998 amendment located). Searched 'ARM 10.7.108 five years 2024 2025 amendment' -- no amendment located.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed. No longer-term authority, no statutory override, no post-1998 amendment located. ARM 10.7.108(1)'s 5-year cap stands.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains High confidence on Max Contract Term.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required = Conditional: competitive bid (21-day, three calls) OR negotiated renewal capped at 12%/year (MCA 20-10-125(1)(a)-(b)).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Exceptions exist (sole source, emergency procurement, cooperative purchasing / interlocal piggyback, board waiver, state-agency approval, small-purchase threshold) that allow renewal-without-bidding outside the 12% safe harbor; or MCA 20-10-125 was recently amended to alter the bidding/12% structure.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-fetched MCA 20-10-125 at mca.legmt.gov and confirmed full verbatim text including 12% cap; confirmed History line ends 'amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 97, L. 1981' (no 2023 or 2025 amendment in official text). Independently verified verbatim against the OPI-compiled 'School Laws of Montana 2025' (PDF at opi.mt.gov), which carries the identical text and identical 1981-last-amendment History line. Confirmed MCA 18-4-124(1) makes Montana Procurement Act optional for school districts, so general procurement-act exceptions (emergency, sole source) are not automatically available; districts would have to have adopted them. No Title 20, Ch. 10 provision authorizes sole-source, emergency, or cooperative-purchasing carve-outs from the 20-10-125 bid-or-12%-negotiate rule. Note: an early WebSearch summary suggested a 2023 'HB 344' amended 20-10-125 with a July 1, 2023 effective date; the official mca.legmt.gov text and the OPI 2025 School Laws PDF both contain no such amendment. HB 344 (2023) was a school-bus-safety bill, not a 20-10-125 amendment. The summary was an unreliable search inference and is rejected.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed. No exceptions to the bid-or-12%-negotiate rule located. No post-1981 substantive amendment to 20-10-125.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains High confidence on Rebid Required; explicitly rejects the WebSearch-summary 'HB 344 2023 amendment' inference.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics = only the 12%/year negotiated-renewal cap (MCA 20-10-125(1)(b)); no CPI/fuel/index escalator and no automatic/evergreen rollover.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A CPI/fuel adjustment, automatic rollover, or state-agency renewal-approval provision exists elsewhere in Title 20, Ch. 10 or ARM Ch. 10.7 that supplements 20-10-125(1)(b).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Read MCA Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 full sections index. Re-fetched MCA 20-10-124 (silent on escalators / rollover). Re-fetched ARM 10.7.108 and ARM 10.7.105 via Cornell LII (signing-before-budget and July-1 individual-contract signing requirements only). No CPI / fuel / index / evergreen / automatic-rollover language located. Confirmed 20-10-125 itself imposes the published-notice-1-week-prior requirement on negotiated renewals (slightly fuller mechanics than Stage 1 recited; added to renewalMechanics value).
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed and slightly enriched. The only renewal-without-rebid mechanism is the 12%-per-year cap, subject to a public meeting at which patrons may appear and be heard and to a 1-week-prior published notice of that meeting.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains High confidence on Renewal Mechanics.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-education transportation follows the same Title 20, Ch. 10 / ARM 10.7 procurement framework; no separate special-ed contracting rule.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student, or disability-transportation contracting rule exists in ARM 10.16 (Special Education) or elsewhere that modifies term, bidding, or renewal requirements.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched ARM Chapter 10.16 topical index and Cornell LII Chapter 10.16 listings -- chapter addresses identification, IEP processes, due-process hearings, tuition rates, records, and definitions, with no procurement / contract-term / bidding rule. Verified ARM 37.86.2234 (Medicaid EPSDT specialized transportation) is a Medicaid coverage/billing rule, not a procurement rule. Verified ARM 10.61.207 applies only to the Montana School for the Deaf and Blind, not a general accreditation procurement standard. Reviewed OPI Special Education guide (August 2024) -- no separate special-ed contracting framework located.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed. No separate special-education procurement framework exists; Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 / ARM Ch. 10.7 apply uniformly.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains High confidence on Special Education.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District-level interpretation variance = Unknown (no evidence of variance and no affirmative evidence of uniformity).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Authoritative evidence exists either (a) that districts interpret MCA 20-10-124/125 or ARM 10.7.108 inconsistently, or (b) that statewide application is affirmatively uniform.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched the Montana DOJ AG opinions database (courts.mt.gov/external/ag-opinions). The only located AG opinion touching contract-bus matters is a 1941 opinion on driver daily records -- not a variance indicator. Searched MTSBA model policy materials; MTSBA model policy language recites the 21-day three-calls-for-bids and 12%-negotiation framework verbatim, suggesting widespread district uniformity, but MTSBA model policy is not authoritative evidence of how every district interprets the statute. Reviewed two district policy / model materials (MTSBA 2024 Model Manual; one rural district MTSBA-adopted manual at content.myconnectsuite.com) -- both adopt the MTSBA model language; PDF binary did not render in fetched form for a verbatim quote of one rural manual, so direct verbatim re-quotation is not available, but search-result snippets show identical 21-day/12% language.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed as 'Unknown.' No authoritative evidence of either variance or affirmative uniformity located. MTSBA model adoption is suggestive but not conclusive.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains Stage 1's Unknown finding; not upgrading to 'No' because no statewide authority affirmatively certifies uniformity.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation = MCA 20-10-124 and 20-10-125 (Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1) and ARM Ch. 10.7 Subchapter 1; Montana Procurement Act (Title 18, Ch. 4) is optional for school districts per MCA 18-4-124(1).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A different controlling statute (e.g., a general school-procurement provision in MCA Title 20, Ch. 9, or a local-government contracting statute in MCA Title 7) supplies the term / bid / renewal rule for school-bus contracts; or MCA 18-4-124(1) does not actually make Title 18, Ch. 4 optional for school districts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-fetched MCA 18-4-124 verbatim at mca.legmt.gov -- confirmed 'A political subdivision or school district may adopt any or all parts of this chapter' (optional). Re-fetched MCA 20-9-204 (conflicts of interest / letting contracts -- general, not school-bus-term-specific). Reviewed MCA Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 sections index -- no other section sets a school-bus service contract term / bid / renewal rule. The OPI 2025 'School Laws of Montana' cross-references 20-10-125 to 20-9-204 (conflicts of interest), 20-9-213 (trustees execute contracts), and Title 2, Ch. 3, Part 2 (open meetings) -- consistent with Stage 1.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed. The school-specific Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 framework controls; the Procurement Act is optional.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains High confidence on Statutory Citation.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness: 2025 MCA edition is the current code; MCA 20-10-124 last amended 1997; MCA 20-10-125 last amended 1981; ARM 10.7.108 last amended 6/26/1998.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: MCA 20-10-124 or 20-10-125 was amended in the 2023 or 2025 Montana Legislature, or ARM 10.7.108 was amended after 1998.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-verified MCA 20-10-124 History line at mca.legmt.gov ('amd. Sec. 31, Ch. 22, L. 1997') and in the OPI 2025 School Laws PDF (same). Re-verified MCA 20-10-125 History line at mca.legmt.gov ('amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 97, L. 1981') and in the OPI 2025 School Laws PDF (same). Re-verified ARM 10.7.108 history via Cornell LII ('Effective 8/6/75 ... Amended 1998 MAR p. 1719, Eff. 6/26/98'). Searched 'Montana 20-10-125 amended 2023 2025' -- the only relevant 2023 result was HB 344, which is a school-bus-safety bill (lap-shoulder belt / vehicle specs) and not a 20-10-125 amendment; the official codified history line is dispositive.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding confirmed. No post-1997 amendment to 20-10-124; no post-1981 amendment to 20-10-125; no post-1998 amendment to ARM 10.7.108.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Cited source-host URLs (archive.legmt.gov) are the official current host for the Montana Code Annotated.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: archive.legmt.gov is the legacy host and the current official MCA host is different.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Issued HTTP requests to https://archive.legmt.gov/bills/mca/.../section_0240/... and .../section_0250/... -- BOTH return HTTP 301 redirects to https://mca.legmt.gov/.... Issued an HTTP request to https://leg.mt.gov/bills/mca/.../section_0250/... -- also redirects to https://archive.legmt.gov/... which then redirects to mca.legmt.gov. The canonical current official host for the Montana Code Annotated is https://mca.legmt.gov.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding partially refuted: archive.legmt.gov is not the current canonical host; mca.legmt.gov is. Substantive Stage 1 findings unchanged, but the cited URLs have been updated to the mca.legmt.gov canonical host in the reconciled source ledger.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No impact on substantive findings; improves citation quality.

sourcesReVerified: MCA 20-10-124 -- re-fetched at mca.legmt.gov (canonical host; archive.legmt.gov 301-redirects here); verbatim text and 1997 History line confirmed.

MCA 20-10-125 -- re-fetched at mca.legmt.gov; verbatim text including the 12%/year cap, 21-day three-calls-for-bids, 1-week-prior published notice, and the 20-9-204 cross-reference confirmed; History line ends 1981.
MCA 18-4-124(1) -- re-fetched at mca.legmt.gov; verbatim 'may adopt' language confirmed (Procurement Act optional for school districts).
MCA 20-9-204 -- re-fetched at mca.legmt.gov; conflict-of-interest / letting-contracts text confirmed (cross-reference target of 20-10-125(2)).
MCA Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 sections index -- re-fetched at mca.legmt.gov; confirmed full universe of sections and that no other Part 1 section sets a service-contract term cap.

ARM 10.7.108 (Bus Contracts) -- verbatim text re-confirmed via Cornell LII pathfinder (Mont-Admin-R-10-7-108); contains 'more than one year but not exceeding five years'; AUTH 20-3-106, 20-10-112; IMP 20-10-124, 20-10-143; last amendment 6/26/1998. The official rules.mt.gov gateway URL was used as the citation (verbatim text not extractable by plain WebFetch due to JS rendering, as expected; Stage 1 already so noted).

ARM 10.7.105 (Pupil Transportation Contracts) -- verbatim text re-confirmed via Cornell LII pathfinder (Mont-Admin-R-10-7-105); July 1 individual-contract signing deadline; AUTH 20-3-106, 20-10-112; IMP 20-10-121, 20-10-124, 20-10-143; last amendment 8/15/2008.

OPI Pupil Transportation page (opi.mt.gov/Leadership/Finance-Grants/School-Finance/Pupil-Transportation) -- re-fetched; directs districts to Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 MCA and ARM Ch. 10.7, Subchapter 1.

OPI 'School Laws of Montana 2025' PDF (opi.mt.gov/Portals/182/Page Files/Legal Issues/2025 School Laws of Montana.pdf) -- newly fetched and parsed; carries verbatim 20-10-124 and 20-10-125 text with the same History lines confirming NO 2023 or 2025 amendment.
ARM 37.86.2234 (EPSDT specialized transportation) -- re-fetched via Cornell LII; confirmed Medicaid coverage/billing rule, not a procurement rule.
ARM 10.61.207 -- re-fetched via Cornell LII; confirmed applies to Montana School for the Deaf and Blind residential students; does not modify Title 20, Ch. 10 / ARM 10.7 procurement framework.
sourcesUnreachable: rules.mt.gov gateway pages for ARM 10.7.108 / 10.7.105 / 10.61.207 -- the SOS gateway is JavaScript-rendered and returns only 'Montana SOS' to plain HTTP fetchers; verbatim text was re-confirmed via Cornell LII pathfinder, and the rules.mt.gov gateway URL is recorded as the citation per OPI/SOS convention.

law.justia.com section-level pages for MCA 20-10-125 -- returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; not needed because the official mca.legmt.gov text and the OPI 2025 School Laws PDF independently confirm the verbatim text.

MTSBA-adopted rural district policy manual (content.myconnectsuite.com PDF for Glacier County District 50) -- PDF binary did not render to text via the available fetcher, so a verbatim quote of that specific manual is not on file; WebSearch snippet showed identical 21-day / 12% language.

sourceLedger.citation: MCA 20-10-124 - Private party contract for transportation -- individual transportation contract
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2025 MCA (current code edition published by the Montana Legislative Services Division)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (implementing authority for ARM 10.7.108); Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics (timing -- fourth Monday of June completion); Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) When the trustees contract with any private party to provide transportation to eligible transportees, the private party shall comply with the regulations of the board of public education for the standards of equipment, operation and safety of the school bus, and qualifications of the driver. (2) Any school bus transportation by a private party or individual transportation that is furnished by a district must be under contract... Transportation contracts for the ensuing year must be completed by the fourth Monday of June, except when an eligible transportee establishes residence in the district after the fourth Monday of June... (3) Private party contracts must be completed in triplicate. (4) Individual transportation contracts (parent/guardian) must be completed in quadruplicate. History: ...amd. Sec. 31, Ch. 22, L. 1997.

sourceLedger.citation: MCA 20-10-125 - Bid letting for contract bus -- payments under transportation contract
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics (12%/year negotiated-renewal cap, public meeting, 1-week-prior published notice)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) Before any contract with a private party for the provision of school bus transportation is awarded, the trustees shall either: (a) secure bids by publishing during a period of 21 days at least three calls for bids in a newspaper of the county that will give notice to the largest number of people of the district or in the official newspaper of the county; the trustees shall let the contract to the lowest responsible bidder, and the trustees shall have the right to reject any and all bids; or (b) negotiate a new contract with the current school bus contractor, provided the negotiated contract costs do not exceed by more than 12% per year the basic costs of the previous year's contract. Such a negotiated contract can be entered into only at a public meeting of the trustees at which meeting the patrons of the district may appear and be heard. Notice of the meeting must have been published in a newspaper of wide circulation within the district at least 1 week prior to the meeting. (2) The provisions of this section for awarding a contract for school bus transportation shall be subject to the provisions of 20-9-204. (3) The trustees shall not expend any moneys... unless (a) a contract... has been completed; and (b) such contracted services... have been actually furnished... History: En. 75-7013 by Sec. 290, Ch. 5, L. 1971; amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 362, L. 1973; R.C.M. 1947, 75-7013; amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 349, L. 1979; amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 97, L. 1981.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - State administrative code / Board of Public Education regulation
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 8/6/1975; last amended 1998 MAR p. 1719, Eff. 6/26/1998 (verbatim text re-confirmed via Cornell LII pathfinder; rules.mt.gov is JS-rendered and not extractable by plain fetch)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (5-year cap); Renewal Mechanics (signing-before-budget timing)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) Contracts between districts and bus contractors must be signed prior to the adoption of the budget by the trustees. Such contracts may run for a period of more than one year but not exceeding five years. Whenever a new contract is completed, one copy is retained by the bus contractor, one copy is retained by the district clerk and one copy is transmitted to the county superintendent. AUTH: 20-3-106, 20-10-112, MCA. IMP: 20-10-124, 20-10-143, MCA.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - State administrative code (verbatim text via Cornell LII pathfinder; official rules.mt.gov URL recorded as the citation)
sourceLedger.citation: Mont. Admin. R. 10.7.105 - Pupil Transportation Contracts
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Renewal Mechanics (July 1 signing deadline for individual transportation contracts; contract-presence requirement for warrant issuance and state reimbursement)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) School districts are required to have individual transportation contracts for the ensuing year signed by July 1. (2) Three copies of the contract form are needed: one for the individual or contractor providing the service, one for the district clerk, and one for the county superintendent. (3) The board of trustees is prohibited from issuing warrants on the transportation budget in the absence of a contract. (4) State reimbursement is paid only on claims supported by valid contracts. AUTH: 20-3-106, 20-10-112, MCA. IMP: 20-10-121, 20-10-124, 20-10-143, MCA.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Codified state statute (official) - applicability/exclusion of general procurement law
sourceLedger.citation: MCA 18-4-124(1) - Local government adoption of procurement provisions
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (establishes that Title 18, Chapter 4 Montana Procurement Act does not apply to school districts of its own force; adoption is OPTIONAL); supports conclusion that the school-specific Title 20, Ch. 10 framework controls.
sourceLedger.relevantText: A political subdivision or school district may adopt any or all parts of this chapter and the accompanying rules promulgated by the department.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Codified state statute (official) - cross-referenced contract-letting provision
sourceLedger.citation: MCA 20-9-204 - Conflicts of interest, letting contracts, and calling for bids -- exceptions
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (cross-reference target of MCA 20-10-125(2)); does NOT itself supply a school-bus contract term limit, but governs general trustee contract-letting and conflicts of interest.
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) It is unlawful for a trustee to: (a) have any pecuniary interest, either directly or indirectly, in any contract made by the trustee while acting in that official capacity or by the board of trustees of which the trustee is a member; or (b) be employed in any capacity by the trustee's own school district, with the exception of officiating at athletic competitions... [Continues through additional subsections governing exceptions for professional services and cooperative purchasing.]

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 - Official state-education-department guidance (corroborates verbatim text of 20-10-124 and 20-10-125)
sourceLedger.citation: OPI - School Laws of Montana 2025 (Susie Hedalen, Superintendent of Public Instruction, pursuant to MCA 20-3-105(5))
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Published May 2026; covers Montana statutory law as of the 69th Montana Legislature 2025 regular session
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (publishes 20-10-124 verbatim); Rebid Required (publishes 20-10-125 verbatim with the 21-day / 12% rule); Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; currentness check (confirms NO 2023 or 2025 amendment to 20-10-124 or 20-10-125).
sourceLedger.relevantText: Carries the verbatim text of MCA 20-10-124 (History line ending 'amd. Sec. 31, Ch. 22, L. 1997') and MCA 20-10-125 (History line ending 'amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 97, L. 1981') as currently in force; cross-references 20-10-125 to 20-9-204 (Conflicts of interest, letting contracts, calling for bids), 20-9-213 (Trustees to execute all contracts), and Title 2, Ch. 3, Part 2 (Open meetings).

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 - Official state-education-department guidance (pathfinder, no new substantive rule)
sourceLedger.citation: Montana Office of Public Instruction - Pupil Transportation
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (directs districts to Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1 MCA and ARM Chapter 10.7, Subchapter 1); District Variance (centralized OPI administration via uniform TR-1/TR-4/TR-5 forms)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Page directs users to Montana Code Annotated: School Buses and Transportation - Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1, and to ARM Chapter 10.7, Subchapter 1. Lists TR-1 Bus Route Reporting, TR-4 Individual Contracts Reporting, and TR-5 Individual Contract Reimbursement instructions.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Table of Contents / statutory index (official)
sourceLedger.citation: Montana Code Title 20, Chapter 10, Part 1 - School Buses and Transportation
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms full universe of statutory school-bus provisions (sections 20-10-101 through 20-10-148); supports negative finding that no other section sets a service-contract term cap or imposes additional rebid requirements.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Lists sections including 20-10-110 (School bus PURCHASE -- installment contracts up to 3 years -- vehicle acquisition, not services), 20-10-121 (Duty of trustees to provide transportation), 20-10-124, 20-10-125, 20-10-126 (Transportation service areas; cooperative service-area agreements valid for current school year), 20-10-143 (Budgeting for transportation and transmittal of transportation contracts). No section in this Part sets a service-contract term ceiling beyond what ARM 10.7.108 imposes.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - State administrative code (negative confirmation - no separate special-ed contracting rule)
sourceLedger.citation: Mont. Admin. R. 37.86.2234 - EPSDT, School-Based Specialized Transportation Services
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special Education (negative): confirms this is a Medicaid coverage/billing rule, NOT a procurement / contract-term / bidding rule.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Coverage of specialized transportation is limited to school-based transportation of clients with disabilities for the purpose of obtaining nonemergency medical services covered by the Medicaid program. Establishes medical-necessity criteria, transportation-type restrictions, and billing conditions; contains no language addressing how districts procure, bid, or structure transportation services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Mont. Admin. R. 10.61.207 - Student Transportation (Montana School for the Deaf and Blind)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special Education (negative): applies only to residential students of the Montana School for the Deaf and Blind; does not modify Title 20, Ch. 10 / ARM 10.7 framework for district-contractor transportation.
sourceLedger.relevantText: The board establishes as policy that the administration of the Montana school for the deaf and blind shall provide for each residential student the most cost-effective and convenient method of transportation to and from home each scheduled travel weekend during the time when school is in session.

queriesThatMattered: Direct re-fetch of MCA 20-10-124 / 20-10-125 / 18-4-124 / 20-9-204 / Title 20 Ch. 10 Part 1 sections index at mca.legmt.gov
Cornell LII verbatim re-confirmation of ARM 10.7.108, ARM 10.7.105, ARM 37.86.2234, ARM 10.61.207
Fetch and parse of OPI 'School Laws of Montana 2025' PDF for independent verbatim corroboration and currentness
Montana Code 20-10-125 amended 2023 2025 bid letting school bus -- searched for any post-1981 amendment
Montana attorney general opinion school bus transportation contract (Montana DOJ AG opinions database)
MTSBA model policy 8120 transportation bidding 12% Montana school district
Montana special education transportation contract IEP procurement separate rule ARM 10.16
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: 2025 MCA edition is the current code. The official mca.legmt.gov host (canonical; archive.legmt.gov 301-redirects here) carries the in-force text. MCA 20-10-124 History line ends 'amd. Sec. 31, Ch. 22, L. 1997'. MCA 20-10-125 History line ends 'amd. Sec. 1, Ch. 97, L. 1981'. Independently re-confirmed against the OPI-compiled 'School Laws of Montana 2025' PDF (covering acts of the 69th Legislature 2025 regular session) -- both sections appear verbatim with the same History lines, confirming NO 2023 or 2025 substantive amendment.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: ARM 10.7.108 effective 8/6/1975; last amendment 1998 MAR p. 1719, Eff. 6/26/1998. ARM 10.7.105 effective 8/6/1975; last amendment 2008 MAR p. 1692, Eff. 8/15/2008. Both confirmed via Cornell LII verbatim republication of the Board of Public Education's official rule text (rules.mt.gov is JS-rendered and not extractable by plain WebFetch; the rules.mt.gov gateway URL is recorded as the official citation).
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Searched the Montana Legislature bill database (archive.legmt.gov) and the OPI 2025 School Laws compilation (covering the 69th Legislature 2025 regular session) for any post-1981 amendment to MCA 20-10-125 or post-1997 amendment to MCA 20-10-124. NONE identified. A WebSearch summary suggesting 2023 HB 344 amended 20-10-125 with a July 1, 2023 effective date was investigated and REJECTED: HB 344 (2023) was a school-bus-safety bill (vehicle equipment / lap-shoulder belt specifications), not a 20-10-125 amendment. The official mca.legmt.gov History line and the OPI 2025 School Laws History line both end 1981, which is dispositive. A 2021 LC draft (LC2336) addressing 'clarifying bid letting requirements in school bus transportation' was not enacted (no resulting amendment appears in the codified text). No 2023 or 2025 session-law amendment to 20-10-124, 20-10-125, or any other Part 1 service-contract provision was identified.
negativeSearchPaths: District variance: searched Montana DOJ Attorney General opinions database (courts.mt.gov/external/ag-opinions and dojmt.gov/attorney-generals-office/attorney-general-opinions); OPI 'School Finance Compass' bulletins (content.govdelivery.com/accounts/MTOPI); MTSBA model policy materials (mtsba.org/sdpolicymanuals); and sfsd.mt.gov Local Government Services audit compliance materials. The only located AG opinion touching contract-bus matters (Opinion No. 310, 1941) addresses driver daily records, not contract-term / bidding / renewal interpretation. MTSBA model policy adopts the 21-day / 12% framework, suggesting widespread district uniformity, but is not authoritative evidence of how every district interprets the statute. Result: Unknown.

Special-education-specific contracting rule: searched MCA Title 20, Chapter 7, Part 4 (Special Education for Exceptional Children); ARM Chapter 10.16 (Special Education) topical index via Cornell LII; ARM 10.61.207 (Montana School for the Deaf and Blind student transportation); ARM 37.86.2234 (Medicaid EPSDT specialized transportation); OPI Special Education guide (August 2024). None modifies the contract-term, bidding, or renewal rules in MCA 20-10-124/125 or ARM 10.7.108 for special-education transportation. Result: same rules apply.

CPI/fuel/index escalator and evergreen renewal: no statutory or administrative provision authorizing automatic CPI, fuel, or index adjustment, or automatic/evergreen rollover, was located in Title 20, Chapter 10 MCA or ARM Chapter 10.7. The only statutory renewal-without-rebid mechanism is the 12%/year negotiated-renewal cap in MCA 20-10-125(1)(b), subject to a public meeting at which patrons may appear and be heard and to a 1-week-prior published notice of the meeting.

Sole-source / emergency-procurement / cooperative-purchasing carve-outs from the bid-or-12%-negotiate rule: none located in MCA Title 20, Ch. 10, Part 1 or ARM Chapter 10.7. The Montana Procurement Act (Title 18, Ch. 4) and its sole-source / emergency exceptions are not automatically available to school districts because MCA 18-4-124(1) makes the Procurement Act optional for school districts; a district would have to have affirmatively adopted those provisions.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No authoritative conflict identified between the school-specific contracting framework (MCA Title 20, Ch. 10; ARM Ch. 10.7) and the Montana Procurement Act (MCA Title 18, Ch. 4), because MCA 18-4-124(1) expressly makes the Procurement Act OPTIONAL for school districts. Where ARM 10.7.108 fixes a 5-year cap and the statute it implements (MCA 20-10-124) is silent on contract length, the rule is a permissible exercise of the Board of Public Education's authority under MCA 20-3-106 and 20-10-112 (the rule's stated AUTH), and no contrary statutory provision or AG opinion was located. The 5-year cap is therefore administrative-code-grounded, not statute-grounded. The cited host URLs were partially refuted (archive.legmt.gov is the legacy host; mca.legmt.gov is the current canonical host); the source ledger now uses mca.legmt.gov.
